channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: ViLegalNLI: Natural Language Inference for Vietnamese Legal Texts
item.description: arXiv:2605.00116v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this article, we introduce ViLegalNLI, the first large-scale Vietnamese Natural Language Inference (NLI) dataset specifically constructed for the legal domain. The dataset consists of 42,012 premise-hypothesis pairs derived from official statutory ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, reasoning, instruction, analysis, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cultural Benchmarking of LLMs in Standard and Dialectal Arabic Dialogues
item.description: arXiv:2605.00119v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: There is a significant gap in evaluating cultural reasoning in LLMs using conversational datasets that capture culturally rich and dialectal contexts. Most Arabic benchmarks focus on short text snippets in Modern Standard Arabic (MSA), overlooking the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, context, experiment, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Why Do LLMs Struggle in Strategic Play? Broken Links Between Observations, Beliefs, and Actions
item.description: arXiv:2605.00226v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly tasked with strategic decision-making under incomplete information, such as in negotiation and policymaking. While LLMs can excel at many such tasks, they also fail in ways that are poorly understood. We s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, prompt, experiment, LLM, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FedACT: Concurrent Federated Intelligence across Heterogeneous Data Sources
item.description: arXiv:2605.00011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative intelligence across decentralized data source devices in a privacy-preserving way. While substantial research attention has been drawn to optimizing the learning process for an individual task, real-world ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, arxiv, experiment, alignment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: What Physics do Data-Driven MoCap-to-Radar Models Learn?
item.description: arXiv:2605.00018v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data-driven MoCap-to-radar models generate plausible micro-Doppler spectrograms, but do they actually learn the underlying physics? We introduce a physics-based interpretability framework to answer this question via two proposed complementary metrics:...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, transformer, analysis, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AirFM-DDA: Air-Interface Foundation Model in the Delay-Doppler-Angle Domain for AI-Native 6G
item.description: arXiv:2605.00020v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The success of large foundation models is catalyzing a new paradigm for AI-native 6G network design: wireless foundation models for physical layer design. However, existing models often operate on channel state information (CSI) in the space-time-freq...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, arxiv, experiment, augmented, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop Meta Bayesian Optimization for Fusion Energy and Scientific Applications
item.description: arXiv:2605.00068v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Inertial Confinement Fusion (ICF) holds transformative promise for sustainable, near-limitless clean energy, yet remains constrained by prohibitively high costs and limited experimental opportunities. This paper presents Human-in-the-Loop Meta Bayesia...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, experiment, model, arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Soft-MSM: Differentiable Context-Aware Elastic Alignment for Time Series
item.description: arXiv:2605.00069v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Elastic distances like dynamic time warping (DTW) are central to time series machine learning because they compare sequences under local temporal misalignment. Soft-DTW is an adaptation of DTW that can be used as a gradient-based loss by replacing the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, experiment, alignment, tool, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Smart Ensemble Learning Framework for Predicting Groundwater Heavy Metal Pollution
item.description: arXiv:2605.00056v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Groundwater in the Densu Basin is increasingly threatened by heavy metal contamination, but conventional methods fail to capture the statistical complexity and spatial heterogeneity of pollution indicators. A key challenge is modelling the Heavy Metal...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, framework, model, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning physically grounded traffic accident reconstruction from public accident reports
item.description: arXiv:2605.00050v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traffic accidents are routinely documented in textual reports, yet physically grounded accident reconstruction remains difficult because detailed scene measurements and expert reconstructions are scarce, costly and hard to scale. Here we formulate acc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, ICL, analysis, framework, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Information-Theoretic Generalization Bounds for Stochastic Gradient Descent with Predictable Virtual Noise
item.description: arXiv:2605.00064v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Information-theoretic generalization bounds analyze stochastic optimization by relating expected generalization error to the mutual information between learned parameters and training data. Virtual perturbation analyses of SGD add auxiliary Gaussian n...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
